stories.headline: US Supreme Court paves way for dismissal of Steve Bannon conviction The order allows a lower court to consider dismissing the former Trump strategist's indictment.
stories.summary: The US Supreme Court has paved the way for the dismissal of Steve Bannon's conviction, allowing a lower court to consider dropping the indictment. The government had filed a motion to dismiss the case in the lower federal court, which will now take up the matter again. The Supreme Court threw out a lower court's decision to uphold Bannon's conviction, clearing the way for the government to pursue dismissal. The Justice Department had asked the district court to dismiss the criminal case against Bannon, a former Trump strategist. The Supreme Court's order vacates a prior appeals court ruling that upheld the conviction, sending the case back to a lower court for further consideration. This development may lead to the dismissal of the charges against Bannon. The case will now proceed in the lower court.

stories.headline: Trump outlines four-hour blitz of destruction if Iran doesn’t make a deal The US president brushed aside concerns about attacking civilian infrastructure and said the Iranian people wanted American bombing to continue.
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump threatened to unleash a four-hour blitz of destruction on Iran if it doesn't make a deal. He disregarded concerns about attacking civilian infrastructure, claiming the Iranian people wanted American bombing to continue. Trump made the comments during a White House news conference, where he discussed a US rescue mission for an airman in Iran. He warned Iran to make a deal "before it is too late," threatening major attacks on Tehran's infrastructure, including bridges and power plants. The threat came after US-Israeli airstrikes took out Iran's tallest bridge, with Trump celebrating the strike on social media. An Iranian leader responded, stating that striking civilian infrastructure would not compel Iranians to surrender. Trump's deadline for a deal is Tuesday, with mixed messages being sent between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Couple die in each other's arms after getting trapped in car during Argentina storm Mariano Robles and Solana Albornoz were discovered locked in an embrace in their white Nissan Versa as they tried to return home from a wedding in Tafi Viejo, n
stories.summary: Mariano Robles and Solana Albornoz, a couple, were found dead in each other's arms after their car was swept away by a flash flood during a storm in Argentina. They were trying to return home from a wedding in Tafi Viejo when the incident occurred. The couple's white Nissan Versa was discovered with them locked in an embrace, suggesting they had been holding each other as the floodwaters rose. The storm caused the flash flood, which trapped the couple in their vehicle, resulting in their deaths. The incident highlights the severe weather conditions in the region, which can lead to tragic consequences. The couple's deaths have been reported, with details emerging about the circumstances surrounding the incident.

stories.headline: ETHIOPIA: Catholic Caritas Ethiopia Holds Its 27th Ordinary General Assembly By Bezawit Assefa Catholic Caritas Ethiopia (CCE), the coordinating office for the social and development activities of the Ethiopian Catholic Church, convened
stories.summary: Catholic Caritas Ethiopia (CCE) held its 27th Ordinary General Assembly on March 27, 2026, at its General Secretariat. The assembly, led by His Eminence Cardinal Berhaneyesus, President of CCE, included comprehensive discussions on key agenda items. The organization reviewed its past year's performance and outlined future plans, highlighting its extensive reach through hundreds of projects focused on education, health, and humanitarian support. The assembly also saw the official unveiling of the organization's new name and logo. CCE is the coordinating office for the social and development activities of the Ethiopian Catholic Church, and its gathering aimed to solidify the Church's commitment to national reconciliation and development. The event is part of the Church's efforts to support millions of people through its various projects and initiatives.

stories.headline: MTG Players Discover Lowered Mystical Archive Rates Thanks To $30 Reprint With several exciting new Mystical Archive reprints revealed, MTG players are realizing how much the mythic rates have been lowered this time
stories.summary: MTG players have discovered that the mythic rates for Mystical Archive reprints have been lowered. The Mystical Archive cards appear at a rate of one per booster pack, guaranteed, which is unusual for a Standard-legal set with a large print run. This change was noticed with the recent reveal of new Mystical Archive reprints. The set includes 252 cards, with both regular and foil versions. Players are taking note of the altered rates, which may impact the overall distribution and prices of the cards. The prices for the Mystical Archive cards were initially released in April 2021, and players are now comparing them to the new reprints. The lowered mythic rates may affect the value and availability of the cards in the set.

stories.headline: Sonar Tech on Smartwatches Enables Hand-Tracking Leap ITHACA, N.Y. – New first-of-its-kind wearable technology from researchers at Cornell University and the Korea Advanced Institute of Science and
stories.summary: Researchers at Cornell University and the Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology have developed a wearable technology called WatchHand, which equips off-the-shelf smartwatches with AI-powered micro sonar. This innovation enables the tracking of hand movements, bringing assistive technologies closer to reality. The system has the potential to support individuals with disabilities and could also be applied to monitoring systems for assisted living and eldercare. WatchHand uses data mining techniques, such as statistical classification and neural networks, to glean valuable information from collected data. This breakthrough could enhance the capabilities of wearable devices, which have been limited by factors such as battery capacity. The development of WatchHand marks a significant advancement in wearable technology, with potential applications in biomedicine and ambient assisted living.

stories.headline: Mechanical Inputs Boost Quantum Sensor States Most people think of diamonds as high-end adornments. Not Ania Bleszynski Jayich. The UC Santa Barbara physicist sees diamonds, which she grows in
stories.summary: UC Santa Barbara physicist Ania Bleszynski Jayich is developing novel sensing techniques using diamonds grown in the UC Quantum Foundry. She sees diamonds as a potentially powerful foundation for quantum sensors, which are currently more advanced than other quantum applications. Jayich, who co-directs the UC Quantum Foundry, envisions diamonds as the key material for advanced quantum sensors, rather than just precious gemstones. Her work focuses on harnessing the unique properties of diamonds to create highly sensitive sensors. With sensors being further along in development than other quantum technologies, Jayich's research is at the forefront of the quantum revolution, exploring the potential of diamonds to drive innovation in quantum sensing. Her efforts aim to shed light on biological, condensed matter, and quantum mechanical systems using these advanced sensors.

stories.headline: NASA's Artemis II crew is already going viral from space NASA's Artemis II crew is going viral in space thanks to iPhone selfies, Instagram Reels, and a "Full House"-inspired video.
stories.summary: NASA's Artemis II crew is gaining attention on social media with their posts from space. The astronauts, including Commander Victor Glover, Reid Wiseman, Jeremy Hansen, and Christina Koch, have shared photos and videos, including a "Full House"-inspired video and iPhone selfies. Using an iPhone 17 Pro Max, they took a high-quality photo of Earth from inside the Orion spacecraft, which has gone viral. The crew has also shared images of Earth on NASA's Instagram, showcasing the planet's beauty from space. The Artemis II mission marks a historic milestone, being the first astronaut mission to the moon since 1972 and featuring the first woman and Black man in deep space. The crew's social media posts are providing a unique glimpse into their journey.

stories.headline: County Championship: Somerset draw with holders Nottinghamshire Holders Nottinghamshire face few alarms as they bat out the afternoon to draw with Somerset in their opening County Championship game.
stories.summary: Somerset and Nottinghamshire drew their County Championship Division One match at the Cooper Associates Ground. The Taunton pitch proved to be a high-scoring one, with Somerset declaring their second innings on 407-5. James Rew scored 122 from 184 balls, including 13 fours and 2 sixes, while Tom Kohler-Cadmore posted 104, his fourth hundred for Somerset. Nottinghamshire, the defending champions, batted out the afternoon to secure the draw, facing few alarms. The result means both teams maintain their title hopes. The match ended in a draw, with the pitch being the only clear winner due to its high-scoring nature. Somerset's declaration set Nottinghamshire a target, but they chose to bat out the day, ensuring the match ended without a winner.

stories.headline: Alvaro Arbeloa refutes claims Kylian Mbappe & Jude Bellingham hinder Real Madrid as coach insists star duo are an 'extraordinary privilege'
stories.summary: Real Madrid manager Alvaro Arbeloa has rejected claims that integrating Kylian Mbappe and Jude Bellingham into the starting XI could disrupt the team's tactical balance. Ahead of the Champions League clash with Bayern Munich, Arbeloa defended the duo, stating it's an "extraordinary privilege" to have players like Mbappe on the team. Mbappe is fit again and set to feature in the Bayern tie. Arbeloa's comments come as the team prepares for a crucial match at the Bernabeu. The manager's stance suggests he values the contributions of Mbappe and Bellingham, who are key players for the team. With Mbappe's return to fitness, Real Madrid is expected to field a strong lineup against Bayern Munich in the Champions League. The match is a significant test for the team, and Arbeloa's tactics will be under scrutiny.

stories.headline: Ipswich Town 2-1 Birmingham City - Kieran McKenna's reaction
stories.summary: Ipswich Town secured a 2-1 comeback win over Birmingham City at Portman Road, boosting their promotion hopes. Birmingham had an equaliser controversially disallowed, with manager Chris Davies feeling his side deserved at least a point. Ipswich boss Kieran McKenna disagreed, believing his team deserved the win. The match was part of the 2025/26 Championship season, which kicked off on Friday evening. The win marks a strong start for Ipswich, who are looking to maintain their position in the Championship. Birmingham, newly promoted, are seeking to emulate Ipswich's success and avoid relegation. The controversial disallowed goal was a key talking point, with both managers offering differing opinions on the decision. The result leaves Ipswich with a positive start to the season, while Birmingham will look to bounce back from the defeat.

stories.headline: Celtics Owner Claps Back at 'Cost-Cutting' Accusation Boston Celtics owner Bill Chisholm knows how it looks--he came in, and the team cut payroll. But there's more to it, he says.
stories.summary: Boston Celtics owner Bill Chisholm has addressed accusations of cost-cutting after the team reduced payroll following his takeover. Chisholm's group finalized the sale of the franchise this summer and subsequently traded away key players Kristaps Porzingis and Jrue Holiday, while also letting Luke Kornet and Al Horford leave in free agency. The moves sparked concerns that the new ownership group would be hesitant to spend money to compete for titles. However, Chisholm has vowed to "do whatever it takes" to win championships, suggesting that the team's offseason moves were part of a larger strategy. He also discussed the potential for a WNBA team in Boston and the challenges posed by the salary cap. Chisholm's comments aim to reassure fans about his commitment to the team's success.

stories.headline: India clears a major nuclear milestone as Kalpakkam fast breeder reactor turns 'critical'
stories.summary: India's 500 MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam has attained criticality, a significant milestone in the country's nuclear program. Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced the achievement, marking India's entry into the second stage of its three-stage nuclear power program. The PFBR is an indigenously built reactor, and its criticality is a crucial step towards generating electricity. The reactor is expected to start fuel loading next week. The achievement demonstrates India's progress in nuclear energy and paves the way for the development of more advanced reactors. The three-stage nuclear program aims to utilize the country's thorium reserves to generate electricity, reducing dependence on uranium imports. The successful attainment of criticality by the PFBR is a major breakthrough in India's nuclear energy sector.

stories.headline: Rajya Sabha Chairman, Lok Sabha Speaker reject Opposition notice seeking CEC’s removal
stories.summary: Rajya Sabha Chairman and Lok Sabha Speaker have rejected opposition notices seeking the removal of Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar. The notices, signed by 63 Rajya Sabha members and 130 Lok Sabha members, were submitted on March 12. According to Section 3, the Speaker and the Rajya Sabha Chairman have the power to admit or refuse such a motion after due consultation and consideration of available material. Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla and Rajya Sabha Chairman C P Radhakrishnan rejected the notices, bringing an end to the first-ever attempt in Parliament to remove a Chief Election Commissioner. A Lok Sabha bulletin stated that the Speaker refused to admit the motion after due consideration, effectively ending the opposition's bid to remove CEC Gyanesh Kumar. The decision was made after considering the available material and consulting with relevant parties.

stories.headline: Even Single Instance Of Award Of Public Work Through Tainted Process Violates Article 14 : Supreme Court
stories.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that even a single instance of awarding public work through a tainted process violates Article 14. The Court emphasized that a level playing field is the foundation of public tenders, and once terms are fixed, they bind both the procurer and bidder alike. The Delhi High Court had set aside a contract award due to altered tender conditions that accommodated specific bidders after the bidding process began. The Supreme Court's decision is based on constitutional principles and statutory frameworks, which subject public procurement to judicial scrutiny. The Court has consistently held that Article 14, which ensures equality and non-arbitrariness, is a cornerstone of the principle against arbitrary state action, and its protection is essential to safeguard the basic structure of the Constitution. This ruling reinforces the importance of transparency and fairness in public procurement processes.

stories.headline: West Bengal SIR: Supreme Court declines Bengal’s plea to delay voter roll freeze
stories.summary: The Supreme Court has declined West Bengal's plea to delay the freeze on voter rolls. The state had 60 lakh voter claims pending, with 27 lakh marked as "doubtful" during the electoral roll revisions. The court is set to decide on these claims. Central forces remain deployed in the state amid the ongoing revisions. The Supreme Court has criticized the West Bengal government over its handling of electoral rolls. Meanwhile, long queues have formed at tribunal centers as deleted voters seek reinstatement ahead of the upcoming assembly elections, with protests also being reported. The Election Commission of India has been asked to provide more logistical information to the court. The court's decision is significant as it will impact the upcoming assembly elections in the state.

stories.headline: Geopolitics of the Third World War Examine how the Third World War (geopolitics) reflects a radical shift from traditional world politics to a new multipolar order.
stories.summary: The concept of a Third World War is being discussed in the context of a shift from traditional world politics to a multipolar order. Former Downing Street Chief of Staff Fiona Hill argues that multiple major conflicts, including in Ukraine and the Middle East, constitute a third world war. Polls show a majority of respondents in the US, Canada, France, and Britain believe World War 3 is likely to break out within the next five years. Iran and the Shiite world are at the forefront of resistance to the West, while North Korea openly supports Russia in Ukraine. Latin America is fragmented, with Brazil leaning towards multipolarity and Argentina supporting the American-Israeli axis. China's strategic thinking is also shifting, with intellectuals discussing grand geopolitics and global capitalist elites, and Beijing potentially planning to strike at Taiwan.

stories.headline: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon annual letter cites risks in geopolitics, AI, private markets
stories.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon's annual letter to shareholders highlights several risks facing the global economy. Dimon cites geopolitics, artificial intelligence, and private markets as potential headwinds. He specifically mentions global conflicts, including ongoing wars in Iran and Ukraine, as top threats to geopolitical stability. Additionally, Dimon notes concerns over persistent inflation, private market upheaval, and what he considers "poor bank regulations." Despite a resilient US economy, Dimon warns that it is not immune to these risks. The letter outlines the potential disruptions caused by AI and the impact of geopolitical tensions on the economy. Dimon's comments provide insight into the challenges facing the global financial system and the need for vigilance in navigating these risks. The letter is part of Dimon's annual assessment of the economic landscape and its implications for JPMorgan Chase.

stories.headline: Cold front: Inside NATO's race to secure the Arctic
stories.summary: NATO has shifted its focus to the Arctic due to increasing threats from Russian militarization and changing geopolitics. The region, previously neglected, is now a critical front for the alliance. The ColdResponse 26 exercise, a routine military training, demonstrated NATO's ability to assemble and operate a significant Arctic force. Held in northern Norway, the exercise was part of the new Arctic Sentry initiative, preparing Western soldiers for combat in the High North. However, it remains to be seen if NATO can maintain a strong presence in the Arctic while also addressing demands from other regions, including the Mediterranean and the Baltic. The exercise showcased NATO's capabilities, but the challenge of simultaneous force deployments across multiple regions still needs to be addressed. The Arctic's strategic importance has prompted NATO to prioritize its defense and security.

stories.headline: DJIA Index: Macro Meets Technicals at a Turning Point The DJIA Index in 2026 isn’t a simple trend—it’s a complex puzzle. Interest rates, Fed policy, global growth, and geopolitics are all pulling in different direc
stories.summary: The DJIA Index in 2026 is influenced by multiple factors, including interest rates, Fed policy, global growth, and geopolitics. US macroeconomic policies, such as interest rate changes and tax reforms, also impact the DJIA value. The rising politicization of central bank balance sheets and diffuse goals may affect asset holdings and sales. A Q3 collapse in US commercial bank reserves has triggered complex drivers that may persist as headaches for the Fed. Despite geopolitical shocks, developed economies and markets outperformed expectations in 2025, with robust growth, falling inflation, and lower interest rates. The Dow Jones Industrial Average is closely watched, with its value and trends affected by these various factors, making it a complex puzzle to analyze and predict its movement in 2026. The index's performance is being closely monitored by economists and investors.

stories.headline: Modi Slams Congress over Pakistan, Champions Women's Quota
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused the Congress of aligning with Pakistan and neglecting national security during election rallies in Assam. He claimed that while his government carried out "Operation Sindoor" on terror camps in Pakistan, the Congress continued to support the neighboring country's narrative. Modi highlighted the BJP's focus on long-term development and peace in Assam. He also promised to implement a 33% women's quota in Parliament, which would come into effect from the 2029 general elections. Modi appealed to women voters, stating that his government had already spoken to opposition parties about the issue. The Prime Minister's comments come ahead of the Assam Assembly elections, where the BJP is seeking to retain power. Modi's speech emphasized the BJP's commitment to development, national security, and women's empowerment. The women's quota proposal is part of the BJP's efforts to woo women voters.

stories.headline: US President Joe Biden Signs Major Climate Change Bill into Law
stories.summary: US President Joe Biden has signed the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 into law, a major climate change bill. The legislation aims to reduce the federal government budget and promote clean energy investments. It includes tax credit programs for climate and energy initiatives. The law is a key part of Biden's efforts to combat climate change, which he has prioritized throughout his administration. The bill's passage is seen as a significant step towards reducing the country's carbon footprint and promoting sustainable energy sources. Biden has predicted that clean energy investments stemming from the law will have a major impact on the economy and the environment. The law's provisions are expected to undo some of the climate and energy initiatives rolled back by previous administrations.

stories.headline: European Union Reaches Landmark Deal on Climate Change Legislation
stories.summary: The European Union has reached a landmark deal on climate change legislation, agreeing to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 90% from 1990 levels by 2040. This target is legally binding and aims to achieve a climate-neutral EU by 2050. The deal also allows for the purchase of foreign carbon credits to cover 5% of the emissions cuts. The agreement builds on the European Green Deal, approved in 2020, which aims to make the EU climate neutral by 2050. EU environment ministers had previously agreed to make the 2050 net zero emissions target legally binding, and the European Council had agreed on a binding 2040 climate target. The deal marks a significant step towards achieving the EU's climate goals and sets a precedent for global climate action. The provisional agreement is expected to be finalized in the coming months.

stories.headline: India and China Engage in High-Level Talks to Resolve Border Dispute
stories.summary: India and China are engaging in high-level talks to resolve their border dispute. China's top diplomat is visiting India to advance dialogue and ease tensions along the disputed border. The two countries have agreed to restart regular dialogue mechanisms and expand cooperation. A working group has been set up to consult and coordinate on border affairs, aiming to advance demarcation negotiations. The talks come amid ongoing tensions and a significant trade deficit in favor of China. Despite efforts to re-engage on trade and talks, border frictions and geopolitical uncertainty remain a challenge. The negotiations aim to find a resolution to the long-standing border dispute and improve relations between the two nations. The Chinese foreign ministry has confirmed the agreement to restart dialogue and expand cooperation, marking a step towards easing tensions.

stories.headline: [Livemint] Sensex up 800 points, Nifty 50 ends above 22,950; investors earn ₹5 lakh crore- Key highlights from stock market today
stories.summary: The Sensex rose 787 points to 74,106.85, a 1.07% increase, while the Nifty 50 gained 255 points to 22,968.25, a 1.12% rise. This surge resulted in investors' wealth increasing by ₹5 lakh crore. The gains come after a sharp decline in the Indian stock markets two days prior, where the Sensex and Nifty fell over 3% due to rising crude oil prices, Middle East tensions, and foreign investor outflows. The recent rebound has helped the market recover from previous losses, with the Nifty staging a smart recovery of over 250 points from its daily low. The strong rally has boosted investor confidence, leading to a significant increase in wealth.

stories.headline: [Times Now] Stock Market Highlights: Sensex Rises For Third Straight Day, Gains 787 Points
stories.summary: The Indian stock market closed in green for the third straight day, with the Sensex rising 787.30 points or 1.07% to 74,106.85. The Nifty also gained, ending 255 points higher at 22,968. The market breadth remained strong, with an advance-decline ratio of 4:1 on the NSE, indicating broad-based buying. The gains were driven by hopes of a US-Iran ceasefire. Top gainers included Titan and SBI Life, which jumped 4% each. The Sensex and Nifty have been on an upward trend, with the Sensex closing at 74,107 and the Nifty topping 22,950. The positive trend is a result of improved investor sentiment, with the market extending gains for the third consecutive session.

stories.headline: [NewsX] Sensex, Nifty Surge Over 1% After Early Slump As Stock Market Rebounds On Iran-US Ceasefire Hopes, Banking Stocks Lead Rally
stories.summary: The Sensex and Nifty surged over 1% after an early slump, driven by hopes of a ceasefire between Iran and the US. The Sensex ended at 74,107, up 787 points, or 1.07%, while the Nifty 50 ended up by 255 points to 22,968. Banking stocks led the rally, boosted by positive quarterly business updates ahead of the earnings season. The gains were also supported by a powerful overnight rally on Wall Street and strong cues from Asian markets. Additionally, Brent crude oil fell 1% to $105.57 per barrel, contributing to the positive market sentiment. The rebound in the stock market was significant, with the Sensex and Nifty indices driven by easing US-Iran tensions and positive global market sentiment. Energy and IT stocks also saw strong buying, further boosting the indices.

stories.headline: [The Economic Times] Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Tuesday
stories.summary: Indian markets saw their sharpest fall in 10 months on Monday, with Sensex dropping 2,226.79 points and Nifty falling 742.85 points. The decline was driven by global recession fears and a tariff-driven selloff, impacting risk sentiment. However, heavyweights like HDFC Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, and Bharti Airtel aided the markets. Out of 4,497 stocks traded on the BSE, 1,932 witnessed advances, 2,381 saw declines, and 184 remained unchanged. Despite this, Indian equities ended higher on Monday, driven by gains in IT and metal stocks, strong auto sales, and Q1 results. The Nifty 50 and Sensex were dragged down by banks, auto, and consumer stocks due to Iran-Israel tensions, high crude prices, and rupee lows. Global markets remained cautious amid trade uncertainties, with Nifty holding key support and stock-specific action driving turnover and volumes.

stories.headline: Pearson CEO: the AI job apocalypse is a Silicon Valley story. The data tells a different one The loudest warnings about AI killing white-collar work are coming from the one industry feeling the disruption. That's no coincidence or reason to panic.
stories.summary: Pearson CEO Omar Abbosh downplays the notion of an AI job apocalypse, suggesting it's a concern primarily felt in Silicon Valley. The warnings about AI killing white-collar work are loudest from the tech industry, which is itself experiencing disruption. Abbosh notes that while it's hard to predict future job types, it's clear which ones will disappear. He believes Pearson's prospects are bright, particularly in assessing skills and providing lifelong learning. The company earns about 80% of its revenue from education assessments, rather than traditional textbooks. Anxieties over AI job losses are high in tech and finance, but Abbosh's comments suggest the impact may be less severe than predicted. The actual number of jobs lost to AI is difficult to quantify, but Abbosh remains optimistic about the future of work and learning. Pearson is adapting to the changing landscape with a focus on skills assessment.

stories.headline: Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge Sales of bitcoin by prominent holders and executive exits could give hope to the battered crypto sector.
stories.summary: Bitcoin has surged above $70,000, indicating renewed investor interest and optimism in the cryptocurrency market. The move occurred during US afternoon hours on Monday, driven by contrarian buy signals. These signals include the exit of a bitcoin treasury company executive and bearish thoughts from a well-followed longtime bull. The cryptocurrency had previously crashed towards $60,000, climbed back above $70,000, and then slipped again to $67,000. The recent climb above $70,000 may be attributed to hopes of a potential market bottom and optimism over a possible Iran ceasefire. The surge suggests that investors are regaining confidence in the crypto sector, despite recent declines and sales of bitcoin by prominent holders. The market is closely watching for further signs of a potential bottom, with some analysts anticipating a possible turnaround. Bitcoin's price movement is being closely monitored by investors and analysts.

stories.headline: OCR preview: Reserve Bank tipped to hold cash rate despite Iran war shock The Reserve Bank is expected to keep the Official Cash Rate on hold when it releases its Monetary Policy Review tomorrow, despite expectations that the Iran...
stories.summary: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand is expected to keep the Official Cash Rate at 2.25% for a second straight meeting. Governor Anna Breman has indicated that the bank will not rush into raising the benchmark in response to the Iran war. Despite global uncertainty, the Reserve Bank is tipped to hold the cash rate steady when it releases its Monetary Policy Review. The decision comes amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East, with the US and Israel involved in a conflict with Iran. The Reserve Bank's move is seen as a cautious approach, with the bank waiting to assess the impact of the conflict on the global economy before making any changes to the cash rate. The Monetary Policy Review is set to be released, providing further insight into the bank's decision-making process.

stories.headline: Future of Fintech Will be Built on Customer Experience: JazzCash Head of Platform Experience & Operations As part of its ongoing engagement with customers and partners, JazzCash leadership visited key touchpoints across Karachi to gather insights and further
stories.summary: JazzCash leadership visited key touchpoints across Karachi to gather insights from customers and partners. The visit is part of the company's ongoing engagement to improve customer experience and further its operations. The Head of Platform Experience & Operations emphasized that the future of fintech will be built on customer experience. JazzCash has been exploring new technologies and partnerships to enhance its services, including testing a new platform that can be deployed across a wide network of POS terminals. The company aims to improve the experiences of existing customers and reach a wider customer base through innovative solutions and collaborations with fintech firms. Recent appointments, such as Arsalan Lodhi as Head of Merchant Products, also reflect the company's focus on driving digital payments and customer-centric services.

stories.headline: OpenAI advocates electric grid, safety net spending for new AI era
stories.summary: OpenAI has released a set of policy recommendations to address the challenges of the new AI era. The suggestions include creating a public wealth fund, implementing fast-response social safety net programs, and speeding up electrical grid development. The goal is to mitigate the potential upheaval caused by advanced artificial intelligence. OpenAI is calling for investments in AI infrastructure, including upgraded electrical grids to support the increased demand for power. The company also emphasizes the need for robust social safety nets to protect workers who may be displaced by automation. By proposing these measures, OpenAI aims to ensure a smoother transition to an AI-driven economy and minimize the negative impacts on society. The recommendations are part of OpenAI's effort to promote responsible AI development and deployment.

stories.headline: Google Unveils PaLM 2, a More Powerful AI Model to Rival ChatGPT
stories.summary: Google has unveiled PaLM 2, a more powerful AI model designed to rival ChatGPT. PaLM 2 brings significant advancements in multilingual support and reasoning abilities. The model was initially used to power Google's generative AI chatbot, Bard, which was rebranded to Gemini in February 2024. PaLM 2 has undergone improvements, including a significant upgrade in logic and coding capabilities compared to its predecessor, LaMDA. The new model will be available next month on Vertex AI, with 9-billion and 27-billion parameter versions. This launch is part of Google's efforts to enhance its AI capabilities and compete with other AI models in the market, such as ChatGPT. PaLM 2's capabilities have been evaluated and shown to be on par with other large language models.

stories.headline: Meta Releases Llama, an AI Designed to Process and Generate Human-Like Language
stories.summary: Meta has released Llama, a large language model designed to process and generate human-like language. The Llama models are open and generative, capable of running on various hardware and performing multiple tasks. The latest release, Llama 4, includes two new models: Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick. Introduced in April 2025, Llama 4 is part of a family of models that can be used for different applications. Meta's Llama models are designed to be versatile and comparable to other large language models (LLMs). The company plans to continue updating and introducing new developer tools to support the model's use, with more information and upgrades to be announced in the future. The release of Llama 4 is seen as a significant development in the field of artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: Microsoft Invests $10 Billion in OpenAI, Expanding Partnership to Accelerate AI Development
stories.summary: Microsoft has invested $10 billion in OpenAI, expanding their long-term partnership to accelerate AI development. The new agreement strengthens their collaboration, drives innovation, and ensures responsible AI progress. The deal resolves months of negotiations and paves the way for further investments in the artificial intelligence startup. Microsoft's initial investment in OpenAI has yielded a significant return, with a $13 billion investment now valued at $228 billion, a 17.6x return. The partnership aims to navigate the complex interplay between technological innovation, financial requirements, and ethical commitments in AI development. The agreement marks a significant moment in the development of artificial intelligence, solidifying Microsoft's position in the industry and setting the stage for future advancements. The investment will support OpenAI's continued growth and innovation in the field of AI.
